title: expose baseline_price and cost_usd in developer earnings export
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `baseline_price` (sum of `dev_price`) and `cost_usd` (sum of `total_price`) to `/account/earnings` JSON and CSV
- Per-bucket markup math is now `baseline_price + pollen_earned = cost_usd @ markup_rate`
- `pollen_earned` and `markup_rate` already existed, kept as-is
- `/account/usage` intentionally unchanged — breakdown lives on the earnings export only
- New `account-earnings.test.ts` covers JSON shape and CSV header/rows

Pipe (`developer_earnings.pipe`) needs to be deployed to Tinybird staging then prod before this merges, otherwise `baseline_price` / `cost_usd` come back as undefined.

Fixes #10735

gist.headline: Receipts, please
gist.blurb: We've added baseline price and total cost breakdowns to the developer earnings export. Now you can see exactly how your Pollen markup adds up!
gist.summary: Added baseline_price and cost_usd fields to the /account/earnings JSON and CSV exports to clarify markup math.
gist.impact: Developers can now see the exact breakdown of base costs versus earned markup in their earnings reports.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing tiny accountant glasses, reviewing a glowing retro terminal displaying a pixelated spreadsheet with gold coins.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny accountant glasses, reviewing a glowing retro terminal that displays a pixelated spreadsheet with gold coins. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
